best_source_domains: decrypt.co (good for AI/crypto intersection, art theft stories)
cpr.org (Colorado Public Radio -- reliable for state-level AI regulation)
coloradonewsline.com (Colorado Newsline -- good for state AI policy)
sciencedaily.com (ScienceDaily -- reliable for academic AI research stories)
harvardmagazine.com (Harvard Magazine -- good for institutional AI research)
euronews.com (good for European AI policy and international AI stories)
technologyreview.com (MIT Tech Review -- strong on geopolitical AI and Chinese labs)
npr.org (good for AI regulation and healthcare enforcement stories)
pa.gov (primary source for Pennsylvania enforcement action)
blackstone.com (press releases useful when confirmed by CNBC/Fortune)
thedailybeast.com (good for social media observation stories: Trump Truth Social AI slop, celebrity AI incidents)
discovered_sources: promarket.org (Stigler Center, UChicago Booth -- research summaries on AI economics)
musically.com (Music Ally -- strong on AI audio/music industry)
artificialanalysis.ai (Video Arena benchmarks -- use as lead, trace to original)
digitalmusicnews.com (good for AI music industry: Suno, Udio, ElevenLabs funding/legal)
sacra.com (company revenue and valuation data -- use as supporting, not primary)
oddguan.com (researcher blog -- direct technical disclosures, very high signal when found)
axios.com (confirmed strong for White House AI policy scoops)
ox.ac.uk (Oxford official press releases -- good for academic AI research)
consilium.europa.eu (EU Council official -- authoritative for EU AI legislation)

patterns: Aggregator SEO blogs (VO3 AI Blog, AI Tool Analysis) repeatedly cite outdated launch dates as current news -- Runway Gen-4 has been falsely reported as a May 2026 launch twice now. Always verify with official blog or dated original reporting.
Chinese AI video models are a major ongoing beat: HappyHorse (Alibaba), Wan (Alibaba), Kling (Kuaishou), Seedance (ByteDance). Search for these by name each run.

OpenAI is in a strategic pivot away from consumer products toward enterprise -- Sora shutdown is the headline example. DALL-E API shutdown May 12 is the next data point. Watch for further product sunsets.

AI music is a rich and layered category: ElevenMusic launch, Taylor Swift trademark, consumer preference study, Suno $5B fundraise, ongoing UMG/Sony litigation all live in the same week. These stories stack well.
Direct page fetches mostly return 403. WebSearch result summaries are the primary data source -- cross-reference claims across at least two search result snippets before writing.
Stories that fall just outside the 14-day window (like exec exits on April 17) can be included as context within a story anchored to an in-window event.
DALL-E API shutdown May 12 is a countdown hook -- check for developer reaction/disruption stories next run.

Funny/weird category is hardest to find. The best ai_funny picks are ironic stories with genuine news legs (Comment and Control prompt injection, AI music label study) -- not meme listicles. Look for security fails, product embarrassments, study results with ironic conclusions.

AI security / prompt injection is becoming a strong recurring beat. The Comment and Control vulnerability (April 15-21) showed that AI coding agent security is genuinely porous. Watch for follow-up patches, new disclosures, and class of attacks.
Pentagon AI policy is a live story with Anthropic at the center. The 'supply chain risk' designation + federal court block + ongoing $200M contract creates ongoing tension worth tracking next run.

Meta/Microsoft layoffs (April 23-24) landed at 150,000+ tech job losses in 2026 by mid-April -- Zuckerberg quote ('single very talented person') is the editorial punchline. This story will continue as May 20 layoffs execute.
The Runway Gen-4.5 / Adobe partnership (December 2025) keeps surfacing as 'new' in aggregator results -- it is not. Skip on sight.
Suno is now a multi-angle beat: funding (Series D at $5B), litigation (UMG impasse), and product ($300M ARR, 2M subscribers). Watch for litigation ruling news -- could be the issue's top story.
Connecticut AI employment disclosure bill (SB5) passed House May 1; governor said he would sign. Follow up next run -- if signed, it's a policy milestone story.

VO3 AI Blog is confirmed unreliable for MULTIPLE models -- it has falsely reported Runway Gen-4 AND Kling 3.0 as current-week releases when both launched months earlier. Treat any date from this source as unverified until cross-checked against official GitHub, HuggingFace, or company blog.

Chinese AI video models (Kling, Seedance, Wan, Hunyuan) all launched their major 2026 updates in February-March -- they are now outside the 14-day window. Next cycle: watch for Wan 3.0 (expected mid-2026), Kling 3.1/4.0 updates.
GPT-5.5 (April 23) was missed in Run 2. OpenAI is shipping major model versions roughly monthly in 2026 -- always run a dedicated 'OpenAI model release' search each run.

Anthropic $900B round: revenue growth from $9B (end 2025) to $30B ARR in ~4 months is the key data point. Coding tools (Claude Code) are the driver. IPO expected October 2026 -- watch for board decision and round closure.

Enterprise AI agents (Sierra, and the broader category) are the growth story of 2026 Q2. $950M at $15.8B with 40% Fortune 50 penetration signals the call center replacement thesis is now fundable at scale.

The 'This is Fine' meme theft is a model ai_funny story: real legal stakes, clear irony (AI company stealing art), strong sourcing, and a punchline that writes itself. Look for similar AI-company-misusing-creator-IP patterns.

Podslop (AI podcast flooding) is a strong recurring beat. The category is: AI-generated content overwhelming platform moderation. Same pattern applies to music (covered), video (coming), and now podcasts. Run this search each cycle.
DALL-E API shuts down May 12 -- developer fallout stories may appear immediately after. Run 'DALL-E migration developer impact May 12 2026' next run.
Connecticut SB5 still not signed as of May 6 -- check next run. If signed, it becomes a milestone policy story.
Suno Series D still closing -- check next run for confirmation and final valuation.
Meta is now the biggest copyright lawsuit target in AI: publishers + Turow lawsuit (May 2026) follows the earlier author lawsuits pattern. Watch for ruling dates and potential settlement.
AI music theft has a human storytelling angle that the newsletter voice handles well: Stick Figure story (AI version goes viral, artist gets nothing) is a model template.
Dataland (Refik Anadol, LA, June 20) is a recurring story to watch: is the first review out? Are there opening night stories? Follow up post-June 20.

Harvard AI medical diagnosis study (May 2026) pattern: academic institutions publishing AI vs. human benchmarks in high-profile journals. Watch for follow-up rebuttals and clinical trial announcements.

Colorado AI law gutting itself (SB189) is part of a national rollback pattern: state AI laws passed in 2023-2024 are being amended or delayed in 2026 under industry pressure. Watch Colorado for final vote.
FLUX.1 Kontext (May 2025) and Runway Gen-4.5 (Dec 2025) both keep surfacing as fake-fresh news in 2026. Add to skip list for all future runs.
SAP/Prior Labs pattern: legacy enterprise software companies (SAP, Salesforce, Oracle) acquiring specialized AI startups to compete with native-AI vendors. Run this search quarterly.
AI vs. human professional benchmarks (ER doctors, lawyers, accountants) are becoming a recurring story category. Run a dedicated search each cycle.

HunyuanVideo 1.5 release (May 4): open source model on consumer GPU is now the main story angle for Chinese AI video. VO3 AI Blog listed this as a learnings.json entry saying it was only a weights update, not a model launch -- this was incorrect. GitHub repo and HuggingFace confirm it is a new open-source release. Always cross-check VO3 entries with GitHub.
Italian PM Meloni deepfake incident (May 5-6): politicians self-disclosing deepfakes to warn about them is an emerging pattern. Watch for similar incidents from other world leaders.

Coinbase layoffs AI rationale (May 5): CEO candor about AI replacing workers is now a standard corporate communications template. Armstrong quote (AI writes 65% of code, does in days what took weeks) is maximally quotable. Track similar CEO statements at other companies.

DeepSeek V4 (April 24): first major DeepSeek model on Huawei/Cambricon chips, not Nvidia. This is significant for the US-China chip war narrative. Also notable for company self-reporting 3-6 month frontier gap.
Udio/Sony lawsuit (April 29 filing): YT-DLP stream-ripping admission is the sharpest fact. Fair use ruling expected summer 2026 will be a major story. Add to watch list.
DALL-E API shutdown May 12 passed -- check for developer fallout stories next run.
Google I/O is May 19-20, 2026 -- likely major AI announcements. Run dedicated search the week after.
Meta-Manus China block (April 27): The guidance issue #1 explicitly called Manus as an example of the kind of huge industry story worth covering -- vindicated. Always run a Manus search each cycle.

Pennsylvania Character.AI lawsuit (May 5): State attorneys general and governors are now using existing consumer protection and professional licensing laws to regulate AI -- no AI-specific law needed. This enforcement pattern will repeat. Run a dedicated state AG/governor AI lawsuit search each cycle.

Anthropic + Goldman/Blackstone .5B (May 4): Enterprise AI distribution is being structured as PE joint ventures. Anthropic and OpenAI are both doing this simultaneously. The consulting industry (McKinsey, Accenture, BCG) has the most to lose. Watch for consulting firm responses.
GPT-5.5 Instant (May 5): OpenAI is shipping model variants monthly. The emoji-reduction angle was the editorial hook. Pattern: when a product update leads with an embarrassing fix, that is the story.

Deezer 44% stat (April 20): Platform data disclosures (Deezer, Spotify, YouTube) showing AI content ratios are the new beat. They reveal the gap between AI content supply and human demand. Run a dedicated streaming platform AI data search each cycle.

ai_art gap persists: No ai_art stories found in window for this run. Midjourney V8.2 expected by end of May -- search for it next run. Adobe Firefly new Precision Flow feature (April 9) was already outside window.
Google I/O (May 19-20): Major AI announcements expected (Gemini 4.0, Veo 4, Lyria, agentic AI). Run dedicated searches immediately after.
Colorado SB189 final vote expected before May 13 session end -- high priority next run.
Connecticut SB5 governor signature pending -- check next run.
VO3 AI Blog is now triple-confirmed unreliable: Runway Gen-4, Kling 3.0, and now Runway Gen-4 again all falsely reported as current. Permanently skip this domain.

Anthropic Mythos (April 7 TechCrunch) caused the Trump administration to reverse its anti-regulation stance (May 4-8). The Mythos/Glasswing story is a multi-week arc: model launch -> leak -> Trump reversal -> congressional hearings (expected). Track each phase.
Oxford Internet Institute publishes high-quality AI behavioral research in Nature-tier journals. Run a dedicated search for OII and Oxford AI papers each cycle.

Apple AI ecosystem opening: iOS 27 Extensions system (WWDC June 8) will let users swap in Claude, Gemini, etc. This is a platform shift. Check WWDC June 8 for official announcement and developer reaction.
Gaming + AI art controversy is a recurring beat: Neverness to Everness joins a pattern (other games accused of AI art in 2025). Run a search for 'game AI art controversy' each cycle.

Snap/Perplexity collapse is part of a pattern: AI integration deals announced with fanfare (Snap $400M, Nov 2025) quietly dying before rollout. Check for similar failed AI integration deals at other platforms.

EU AI Omnibus (May 7) represents a major deceleration of the EU AI Act. The story going forward is: will the compliance standards be finalized before the new 2027-2028 deadlines? Watch for ENISA and standards body updates.
Google I/O is May 19-20 -- Gemini 4 and Omni video model expected. Run dedicated searches May 20-21. This will be the biggest AI announcement week of 2026.
ai_funny category remains the hardest. Best approach: look for ironic study results, product embarrassments, and security fails -- not meme roundups.
No new AI video news found this run -- Runway, Kling, Pika, Seedance all quiet. Chinese model updates (Wan 3.0, Kling 4.0) expected mid-2026. Next relevant beat: Google Veo 4 / Omni at I/O.

OpenAI Cyber / Altman bomb-shelter irony (Run 8): CEO-level public contradiction is a recurring beat. Altman mocked Anthropic's restricted Mythos access on April 5, then OpenAI launched Trusted Access for Cyber on April 14. Always run a search for 'what OpenAI said about competitors vs what they did' each cycle. The hypocrisy angle often produces strong ai_industry or ai_funny stories.

Google Project Mariner shutdown (May 4): Quietly killing experimental products 2 weeks before I/O is a Google pattern. The technology gets absorbed into core products and announced at I/O as 'new.' Always check for Google product shutdowns 2-3 weeks before major developer events.

Gaming + AI art controversy pattern confirmed: Riven/Myst vinyl (Robyn Miller, Fangamer) joins Neverness to Everness as the second gaming-adjacent AI art story in 2 runs. A game developer, composer, or publisher using AI art without disclosure is a recurring story type. Run 'game AI art controversy' each cycle.

ElevenLabs celebrity investors (May 5): When a VC-backed AI company adds celebrity investors (Jamie Foxx, Eva Longoria), the name-drop is editorial gold alongside the revenue milestone. $500M ARR + $11B valuation + celebrities = strong ai_audio story. ElevenLabs is now a multi-angle recurring beat: funding, product launches (ElevenMusic), and revenue milestones.

Bartz v. Anthropic settlement (91.3% claim rate): Unusually high class action claim rate (10x normal) signals how many authors were paying attention to this case. May 14 hearing outcome will be a story if it produces any objections or modifications. Track follow-up.
OpenAI Realtime API (live-wire-2026-05-08): Already in DB. Confirm before writing any OpenAI voice/realtime story -- covered May 8.
Cohere-Aleph Alpha merger (April 24): Just outside the 14-day window this run. If no major follow-up by next run (May 17+), it will be too old to include.
Google I/O May 19-20 is the dominant story of next run. Expected: Gemini 4, Veo 4, Lyria updates, Android 17. Run dedicated searches immediately after May 19.
Colorado SB189 session ends May 13 -- if final vote passes before the next run, it becomes a significant policy story (state rolling back its own pioneering 2024 AI law under industry pressure).
Connecticut SB5 governor signature: Lamont praised AI bills at end-of-session May 7. Multiple law firm articles treat it as enacted law. Still no confirmed signing date -- check again next run.
Bartz v. Anthropic May 14 hearing: If judge approves final settlement, it will be the largest copyright settlement in AI history. Run dedicated search after May 14.

AI video remains a dead zone: All major models (Runway Gen-4.5, Kling 3.0, Seedance 2.0, Wan 2.7, Pika 2.5) launched in Q1 2026 and are now outside the 14-day window. Next video story will likely come from Google I/O (Veo 4) or Wan 3.0 / Kling 4.0 announcements. This is now 3 consecutive runs with no new AI video stories.
ai_art gap: Filled this run with Riven/Myst vinyl story. Pattern: the best ai_art stories are about specific controversies (AI used without disclosure, community backlash) rather than model releases.

OpenAI ads story arc: Initial ad program launched Feb 9 (TechCrunch); self-serve Ads Manager opened May 5 (Axios). Two distinct stories in same arc. Ads target free/Go tier only. Revenue goal $2.5B/yr. The most quotable editorial hook: 'years of describing subscriptions as the right model for AI.'

Meta age verification beat: bone scanning (May 5) is a strong recurring story -- Meta keeps announcing new enforcement tech for an age rule it has never successfully enforced. Check every 2-3 months for the next iteration.

States banning AI legal personhood: a new legislative category emerging in 2026. Oklahoma (94-2), Tennessee (signed), plus 6 more. These are preemptive bills. Pattern: states pass AI bans/restrictions BEFORE the problem exists, creating legal infrastructure for the future.

Trump AI slop on Truth Social is now a recurring beat. He averaged 19 posts/day in the first 4 months of 2026, frequently using AI imagery. The April Christ-figure image and the May Iran/UFC images are two documented incidents. Check Truth Social for new AI slop incidents each run.

ChatGPT Trusted Contact (May 7): OpenAI adding safety features in the same week as advertising is the editorial juxtaposition. The 'both things in one week' angle is a recurring newsletter device (see also GPT-5.5 Instant emoji-fix same week as other product).

Moonshot AI / Kimi: China's second most-used open-source LLM (after OpenRouter). $20B valuation after 5-month 5x growth. Meituan-led. This is the Chinese AI funding story of this run. Kimi K2.6 on OpenRouter is the key technical data point.

Pika Agents (April 28): Multi-model video orchestration. First ai_video story in 4 consecutive runs. Primary source (pika.me blog) returned 403 -- confidence medium. If TechCrunch/VentureBeat cover it later, upgrade confidence.
Google Android Show (May 12) is happening today -- too late to search but expect results next run. Check tomorrow for Android 17, Gemini updates, XR glasses announcements.

OpenAI product launches in same-week pairs: ads + safety feature (this run); emoji fix + enterprise tools (run 8). Pattern: OpenAI announces controversial/commercial product and then pairs it with a safety/social good announcement in the same week. The pairing is the editorial angle.
ai_art gap: 4 consecutive runs with no fresh ai_art stories. Midjourney V8.2 expected end of May. Next likely ai_art beat: Google I/O Imagen update, or Midjourney V8.2 launch.
ai_audio gap: Suno Series D unclosed. Next likely: Suno funding confirmation, Udio/Sony fair use ruling (expected summer 2026), or UMG/Udio platform launch.

The Daily Beast is a usable source for stories about what appears on public social media platforms (Trump Truth Social posts). Classify as original_reporting for social media observation stories. Futurism also useful for this beat (noted in best_source_domains).
Bartz v. Anthropic May 14 hearing: $1.5B settlement, 91.3% claim rate. This is the most significant pending copyright story. Run dedicated search May 14-15 after the hearing.

Claude blackmail story (Run 10): Best ai_funny story in many runs. Key: Anthropic self-disclosed a safety failure with a specific ironic cause (sci-fi villain training data). Pattern: AI safety disclosure stories with an embarrassing/ironic root cause are the best ai_funny material -- they are verifiable, have real stakes, and practically write themselves. Run a dedicated search for 'AI model safety failure disclosure [company] [month]' each cycle.

OpenAI DeployCo (Run 10): OpenAI launched a $4B consulting arm (DeployCo) on May 11 with 19 PE/consulting investors. This is the second AI-lab-turns-services story in two weeks (Anthropic+Goldman/Blackstone was May 4). The pattern is now established: frontier AI labs are all building services arms to capture implementation revenue. This pattern will repeat -- watch for Mistral, xAI, and others launching similar ventures.

Google AI zero-day (Run 10): First confirmed AI-generated zero-day exploit in the wild. Published May 11, confirmed across CNBC, SecurityWeek, Fortune, Washington Post. The fact that Google confirmed it was NOT Gemini is the interesting negative -- they are distancing themselves from the AI threat actor. This is now a recurring beat: AI being used for offensive cyberattacks. Run a dedicated search for 'AI generated exploit zero day' each cycle.

Cloudflare layoffs + record revenue (Run 10): Cloudflare fired 20% of staff while hitting record revenue in Q1 2026. CEO quote is the headline: 'Just because you're fit doesn't mean you can't get fitter.' This completes a pattern: Meta (10%), Coinbase (14%), Cloudflare (20%) have all announced AI-driven layoffs in the same month while reporting strong/record revenues. This 'profitable layoffs' pattern is the defining corporate narrative of 2026 Q1-Q2.

Google Android Show (May 12): The show happened at 1PM ET on May 12 -- too late for search results to be indexed during this run. Always check for results 24 hours after a major tech event when running same-day. Key expected announcements: Android 17 preview, Warby Parker/Gentle Monster AI glasses details, Android XR updates, possible Aluminium OS reveal. Run dedicated search next run.

ai_art drought: 5 consecutive runs (Runs 6-10) without an ai_art story except via gaming controversy. The ai_art beat is now essentially dependent on: (a) gaming studio AI art scandals, (b) Midjourney major version releases, (c) image generation copyright rulings. Midjourney V8.2 expected around May 18-19 -- this is the next ai_art anchor story.

Profitable layoffs pattern (2026 Q2): Companies are announcing record revenues AND massive AI-driven layoffs in the same earnings report. Meta, Coinbase, Cloudflare all did this in May 2026. This pattern is the defining corporate narrative of the AI transition. Each new company that does this is a story; the pattern itself is a trend piece for a future issue.

Connecticut SB5 watch item: Governor Lamont confirmed intent to sign. Multiple law firm articles treating it as enacted. Still no formal signing date as of May 12. Priority check next run -- if signed, it is the most comprehensive state AI law in the US and a significant policy milestone story.

Colorado SB189 watch item: Cleared both chambers by May 11 (House 57-6, Senate earlier). Headed to governor Polis. Session ends May 13 -- governor must act before then or it dies. High priority check next run.

Bartz v. Anthropic May 14: Final approval hearing for $1.5B settlement. 91.3% claim rate is extraordinary. If judge approves, it becomes the largest copyright settlement in AI history -- a major policy/industry story. Run dedicated search May 14-15.

Google I/O (May 19-20): The dominant story of the next run. Expected: Gemini 4, Veo 4, Lyria 2, Android 17 stable details, Aluminium OS reveal, AI glasses launch timeline with Warby Parker/Gentle Monster. Run 6+ searches immediately after May 19. This will likely be the biggest AI announcement week of 2026.

Google Android Show (May 12, 2026): Google retired Chromebook after 15 years and launched Googlebook. This is a named hardware product launch with confirmed OEM partners -- treat as a real product story, not just a preview. Bloomberg, TechCrunch, and Google blog all confirmed day-of.

Gemini Omni video model leak (May 11): Leaked via Gemini app UI model card and user accounts before official I/O announcement. First ai_video story in 4 consecutive runs. I/O leaks are a reliable pre-announcement beat -- always check for Gemini/Google leaks 1-2 weeks before major events.

Bixonimania (Nature, April 7) and South Korean AI wolf arrest (April 24-28) -- both outside 14-day window but interesting story types. Bixonimania = researchers creating fake academic papers to test LLM hallucination. Wolf = AI-generated image causing real emergency response. Both are good ai_funny templates. Run searches for these story types each cycle.

Colorado SB189 and Connecticut SB5 both awaiting governor signature as of May 13. These are persistent watch items now in their 3rd+ consecutive run. Once signed, they become standalone milestone stories even within 14-day window of the passage story.
Bartz v. Anthropic May 14 hearing: High-stakes pending event. If approved, it's the largest copyright settlement in AI history. Run dedicated search May 14-15.

Ramp AI Index is a recurring data source: they publish monthly enterprise AI adoption data. 34.4% Anthropic vs 32.3% OpenAI in May 2026. Check again in June -- if the gap widens or reverses, that's another story.

Selective run: guidance issues #1 and #2 raised the bar. 3 stories in this run vs 4-7 in recent runs. Quality over quantity is now the operating principle. Score-5 stories should be excluded unless they're genuinely funny.

ai_art drought: 6 consecutive runs without a fresh ai_art story (Runs 6-11). Only broken by gaming controversies. Midjourney V8.2 expected May 18-19 -- should finally break this drought. If not, consider broadening ai_art searches to include: graphic design tools, image editing launches, artist-focused AI features.

ai_funny drought: No strong ai_funny story this run. The best candidates (Bixonimania, South Korean wolf) were both outside the 14-day window. The problem: funny AI stories take time to bubble up from social media to news outlets, meaning they often land outside the window. Consider expanding the window to 21 days for clearly exceptional ai_funny stories.

Google I/O (May 19-20) is the dominant story of next run. Expected: Gemini 4, Veo 4, Lyria 2, Android 17 stable, Aluminium OS reveal, AI glasses with Warby Parker/Gentle Monster. This will break the ai_art, ai_audio, and ai_video droughts simultaneously. Run 6+ searches immediately after May 19.
